name: Miriam's Stuffed Cabbage Aug 1997
recipeIngredient: 4 tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped (or 1 can tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch cabbage in boiling water. Drain and separate 12 leaves. Save the rest for soup.
2. Grind beef in a food processor along with 1 onion, 1 clove garlic.
3. Transfer to a bowl and add salt, pepper, thyme, 1 tablespoon paprika, and rice. Stir to combine. Set aside.
4. Chop the remaining onions and mince the other clove garlic.
5. Cook onion and garlic in oil until onion is soft.
6. Stir in tomatoes and cook until almost a paste. Stir in sugar.
7. Meanwhile, place 2 tablespoons of filling in one side of a cabbage leaf and roll the leaf up, tucking the sides in.
8. Add paprika, salt, pepper, and caraway seeds to the sauce.
9. Place half the sauerkraut in the pan. Arrange the rolls on top of the sauerkraut. Add wine. Top with the remaining sauerkraut.
10. Simmer for 1½ hours, adding a little water when the sauce starts to dry.
11. Stir in cream and serve immediately.
